tokens: We'd like a rough idea of where you are in your EVA Prep at this time.
And we'd like an OPS (pressure) readout at your convenience.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, I'm zipping Jim right now and we'll call you in a little while.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Houston, how do you read the LMP?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just want a comm check, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Our biomed on you is a little noisy.
We're wondering if you're all plugged up properly on that or if it's easy to get to those sensors still.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Is it your biomed there that we're reading now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're getting quite a noisy return down here.
He may want to take a look at those sensors.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And in the meantime, have you put tape over the canisters that are filled with water there (as per the discussion at
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We put the locks back on, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jimmy, we're sitting down here scratching our heads about some data we're getting on your PLSS; and we'd like to know if the PLSS was vertical yesterday evening when you serviced it or if it perhaps were leaning sharply back against something when you serviced it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, when we serviced mine last night it was tilted aft about 30 degrees from vertical, and that's the long dimension was tilted vertical.
(Pause) And we gave that information to Gordo this morning.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Stand by a second; I'll be back with you in a moment here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Houston, it was tilted such that we could get the O2 high-pressure line in it at the same time we were doing the water servicing to save some time.
So you can probably get a picture of the angle of it by considering the PLSS on the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We hate to hold you up here, but we think we have a real problem with a bubble of air in your PLSS water, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And we're going to have to ask you to take a few minutes here and do a feedwater recharge on your PLSS.
And I'll go over the steps which we'd like you to follow when you're ready to copy them.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Joe, rather than copy, let us get the PLSS up in position, and you just talk us through it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can sure do that, Dave.
I'm ready to read when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Next step: LM descent H2O, Close.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Next step: connect waste management system to PLSS Primary vent.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll hook the waste management system to the Primary vent.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Does this happen to be anything like the procedures we have on board already?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, from here on out, they're going to be similar.
I want to give you a step 4 here, when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Connect LM H2O to PLSS H2O Fill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It's going to be line 9 to the end of your checklist PLSS recharge procedures, and I'll read them to you if you want, I have them right here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, we can do it, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, you should be starting with line "LM descent H2O, Open."
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And the line is LM descent H2O, Open.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we'll go ahead from there with the checklist, then.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Follow it right through to the end, Davy; thank you.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Those steps are complete down through the left column of page 5-6.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I guess you pick up business as usual then.
However, Dave, could you put pressure on the lower of your three EKG sensors?
We think that may be the problem with our signal down here.
And that's just pressure on that sensor from the outside.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) And Surgeon tells me it's on the left side, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, you've got pressure on it now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Try pressure on one of the two upper ones, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, which of the upper ones have you touched now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, that's the only way we can get to them right now, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, could you physically move...I guess maybe we've lost the signal or something.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, the pressure's removed right now, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Joe, (as per checklist page 6-2) the OPS checkout has 5700 on mine and 5600 on Jim's.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, the story on the biomed is essentially the following.
We've got a good heart rate on you, or at least readable, when you're perfectly still.
Otherwise, we're not able to pull the rate out of the signal return that we're getting.
And we've talked it over here.
You're going to be the pacing item on this.
We're going to give you a go ahead and rely on your own good judgment to keep us posted on crew status.
We're all aware that this EVA is physically a fairly easy EVA, with the exception of the drill work coming up towards the last.
And we may have more information regarding your biomed sensors.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll cool it all the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And Jim, it is important that we have your biomed data.
Are you plugged into us yet?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, he's switching now; take a look at it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And his sensors are nice and new today.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, did you have any trouble with mine last night, or is it just since I put the suit on?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we may have dinged them when the suit went on.
They looked real fine all night long.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Long Pause) Houston, could you give us a hack on where we stand relative to the time, please?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Could you give us a quick estimate of where you are on your EVA Prep Card.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're halfway through LMP PLSS donning.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'll be back at you in a minute with some numbers.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Long Pause) I guess what I'm asking, Joe, is where are we relative to when you plan for us to come out?
You know our mission timer isn't working; so I'd just like to know when you expected us to come out, so we know how to pace it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, this is Houston.
We estimate you should be climbing out of the LM about an hour from now, which would make it about a quarter to seven, Houston time.
However, we're standing by and whenever you're ready to go, please press on.
We are running a little bit behind the nominal time right now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, my question was, when was the nominal time?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...in front of us, you're climbing out of the LM right now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Jim, be advised your biomed data looks quite clean to us.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I don't know if you copied my last transmission.
Your biomed data is quite clean.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Jim and Dave, while you're working around there, I've got your morning science report.
We've just learned here that the X-ray spectrometer people are about to generate a compositional map of the central belt of the Moon based on Al's data.
You'll also be interested to hear that the pan camera has photographed - at high resolution - the landing site at Hadley Rille.
And we're thinking that when we get those pictures analyzed, if it looks good, maybe we should try landing there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: There's lots more to do up here than we could ever handle.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Looks like you're handling that pretty well.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: In taking a look at things on the PLSS straps, we noticed that Jim's yo-yo had failed yesterday too.
So we'll have to do a little improvising there today.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Getting ready for a comm check with the PLSSs (as per checklist page 6-3) if you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll start out, and give you a call in a minute here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Reading) "Mode to VOX; VOX Sensitivity to Max; VHF A, T/R; and B, Receive." (Now on checklist page 6-4) "CB(16) comm, SE Audio, open, and you connect to the PLSS comm." (Long Pause) Okay.
"CB(16) comm, SE Audio, close PLSS PTT, Maintain, right; verify.
And Mode A, wheel counter-clockwise." (Pause) Okay.
"Tone, On; Vent flag, P; Press flag, O; O2 Momentary; PLSS O2 pressure gauge, greater than 85 (percent)." What have you got?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, a comm check with me and Houston.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, cycle it back, and (is) your SE Audio closed?
Okay, your VOX isn't coming through at all.
(Pause) Okay, let's go back to VHF A Transmitter to Voice.
A Receiver, On; B Transmitter to Off; B Receiver, On.
Okay, give me a call now.
Don't seem to have any side tone or any transmission out of the LMP's PLSS on A.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We copy your comment loud and clear.
We agree, we don't have anything from Jim yet; and we're looking at it now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave and Jim, this is Houston.
We'd like for you first to verify the
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It's checked; but we'll check it again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, could you go back to the audio portion of your cue card there (on checklist page 6-3), Audio-LMP, and check those few steps for us, please?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Jim) Why don't you cycle the circuit breaker?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...we'll go back up one more step and verify the powerdown circuit breaker configuration, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; Jim says you're coming in very weak, Joe.
We'll verify the powerdown circuit breaker (card).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Houston, both of those are verified.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Dave and Jim, could you check the suit connector, please?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Jim's reading you so the suit connector must be connected.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: He can receive, but he can't transmit.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We agree with that; but we assume also that he is receiving us very weakly.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) I'm afraid - ha - to tell you, but Jim's antenna is broken, and it was broken yesterday when we got in.
When we first started out on the operation, there was a big nick out of the antenna, which we subsequently taped.
And now, just below that nick, it's broken off.
And I don't know how it got there, but when we first looked at the OPS antenna, it had about half of it nicked out.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Dave) Yeah; I'm reading you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like we'll have to do a little tape job...(stops to listen to Allen) Joe, what I'm doing now is holding Jim's antenna together.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I guess we'll have to think...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and when you hold it together, we can read you loud and clear.
I guess we need a tape job on that antenna.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I hope I can get it taped for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: In other words, we'll have to tape it and leave it erect.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; let me get the tape out.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's been a taping operation this morning.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we're reading you 5 by, babe, when that thing's connected.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Yeah, I know it, but I don't know how we're going to do that, because it's...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim could you go to Off on your Mode Select switch while you're taping?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And we're standing by for progress, as to how that goes.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think we're going to take the top inch off the antenna and use it as a splice.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's the old splint trick, you mean.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, we'll give that a try, because, unfortunately, the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, we're scratching our heads on that down here; and as you know, all we need really is a small metal-to-metal connection between the broken piece and the root of the antenna coming out.
Dave, you may want to tape the antenna over across the top of the PLSS.
If you think that'll be mechanically more secure than taping it straight up.
We'll rely on you to decide on that one.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's let Jim come back up on comm and lay the antenna across here, and let's see how it works.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and, Dave, while you have the tape out there, from the sound of things, maybe you'd better put...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...some of it in your pocket for later.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Houston) Stand by a minute, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like the best we're probably going to do here is to be able to tape it up so we have the contact, and then leave the PLSS antenna down rather than erecting it.
I'm afraid if we erect it, why, there's not much room to put tape in there, and it may fall off and then we'd be out of business.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll go through it (meaning the LMP comm check on 6-4) again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Squealing) Houston, how do you read the LMP?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'll turn it down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, you have to check with Houston.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, we read you both loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, they read me loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: PLSS mode for you to B and me to A.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, how do you read, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, you're loud and clear to me, and Houston, how do you read the CDR?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're loud and clear to me; how me to you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How do you read the LMP and the CDR again?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're loud and clear on both, and we're Go for the next step.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: My PLSS O2 quantity is 90 percent.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: "Verify ECS caution and H2O SEP component lights on, about a minute." (Pause) And there they come.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you slip around here?
(Pause) That water is cold, isn't it?
"OPS O2 hose to PGA blue to blue." (Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll turn off the LCG Pump.
(Dave laughs, probably because he is getting quite cold) (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: "Retrieve purge valve, verify closed; and lock pin, In and Lo.
Install purge valve red to red."
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Purge valve is in, and locked, and locked.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're going to have to...Yeah, suit disconnect.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get...My O2 hose has just sort of drifted up there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'll disconnect your (LM O2) hoses.
(Pause) And we'll connect the PLSS O2 hose.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, all locked, and the pin's in.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: "PGA diverter valve to vertical." Take a little drink.
(Pause) Did you turn the Descent Water off?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, that Descent Water was turned off on your PLSS recharge exercise.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Put it in the holster and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Vent flag clear." (Pause) Okay, my fan's coming on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay; let me get your water (that is, the in-suit
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, you line it up and I'll push it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Wait, let me get this...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Okay, you watch my water spigot there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You may have to push it some.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Brief Pause) You'll have to rotate the helmet, too.
Shift it just a little bit.
A little more to the right.
Back a little to the left.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay; secure the self-doffing straps.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get back to your little corner.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: "Disconnect LM water hoses and connect the PLSS water hoses."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Push them just as far back as you possibly can, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) Yeah, they're snapped in the ECS handhold.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do a 180 here, and we'll check all the connectors.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me put the booties (that is, the connector dust covers) on here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's take another look at the circuit breaker configuration.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: "White Dots out plus EVA decals." (Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That old graphite makes the hands slide in pretty easy, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughing) It doesn't do too good on the wrist connector though, does it?
It makes it a little bit...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I got two (gloves) on and locked.
Come over and check mine when you get through.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Gimme a call if you want a hand.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Checking Jim's glove) Okay; got a lock and a lock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and "PLSS Pump, On, to the right."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A and B going to Egress.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we need a pressure integrity check.
So next step is "PLSS O2 to On".
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, my PLSS O2 is On.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, and the press flag should clear at 3.1 to 3.4 (psi).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I'm sorry; no; I didn't clear the (tone)...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, there's your tone; yeah, mine too.
(Long Pause) Okay, I'm off the peg.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, if I can get that old O2 valve.
Let's turn them off and do a little check.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, mine (meaning his PLSS O2)'s Off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And, Houston, we'll take your call when the minute's up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Mickey's big hand went above his head.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: "Verify the O2 flag is clear." Mine is clear.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How do things look to you down there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Falcon; you're Go for depress.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, Jim, "CB(16) ECS Cabin Repress, open."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, overhead...I've got a little tone.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Suit circuit's locked up at about 4.4.
My PGA is coming through 5 and decaying.
And let's slip on a watch.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: "Verify tone on and H2O flag at about 1.2 to 1.7." (Pause) Okay, 2.5; 2.0.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Long Pause) And, we're about down to 0.2.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Take a while at this rate.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, your PLSS water recharge cured that problem we had yesterday completely, it looks like.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, it was worth the effort then.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Those tones are a little disturbing.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well; " partially open the forward hatch." Maybe I can get that if you'll slip over there...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...about as far as you can go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Can you reach around and hold the hatch so it doesn't blow shut?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Grunting as he tries to reach his feedwater valve) Oh!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me see if I can get around the hatch here and give you some more room.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, maybe I can get my arm back there.
I've got my arm back there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll tell you, my gloves feel a lot better today.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughs) Stretched them a little bit yesterday, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: "Rest until cooling sufficient", after we get the water going.
(Pause) And I got a tone and a clear water flag.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get that (piece of duct) tape up by your left elbow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I will; in case I drop one.
Stick it right on my cuff checklist.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, my water flag finally cleared.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How does everything look to you down there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Be advised you're Go for EVA.
transcript.speaker: Allen
